Article Title: Mumbai Hit And Run News: Navi Mumbai hit-and-run: 24-year-old biker's head severed after rear-impact crash; pillion critical
Article Text:
A 24-year-old man was tragically killed in a hit-and-run on Palm Beach Road near Sanpada, his head severed due to the severe impact (AI image)

Baramati Crash Row, Shinde Flight Delay, Navi Mumbai Hit And Run Among Top Updates

NAVI MUMBAI: A 24-year-old man was killed in a hit-and-run incident on Palm Beach road near Sanpada on Tuesday.The impact of the crash was so severe that the man was decapitated as his bike skid for about 50 meters.The 19-year-old girl riding pillion was seriously injured. According to police, an unidentified vehicle rammed into the bike from behind and sped away.The deceased, identified as Prashant Jamdade, works for a private firm. The pillion rider, Harman Kaur, sustained severe injuries to her head and both legs and has been admitted in the ICU. PSI Suraj Raut informed that the two friends were returning after celebrating Holika Dahan.Around 3 am, when they reached near Sanpada, the unidentified vehicle rammed into their bike."We checked all the CCTV camera footages along the stretch of road where the mishap occurred to get some lead of the unidentified vehicle that hit the bike. Unfortunately, there is no CCTV camera installed near the accident spot," he said.An FIR has been registered at Sanpada police station against the unidentified driver under BNS and Motor Vehicle Act.
Article Summary: The impact of the crash was so severe that the man was decapitated as his bike skid for about 50 meters. The 19-year-old girl riding pillion was seriously injured. An FIR has been registered at Sanpada police station against the unidentified driver under BNS and Motor Vehicle Act.

Article Title: Elderly Couple Assaulted: Elderly couple thrashed over senior citizens’ seat on bus in Kolakta, accused held
Article Text:
Kolkata: Elderly Couple Assaulted On Bus, Rahul Poll Boycott Call, 104-Year Voter Row And More





KOLKATA: A 25-year-old man was arrested after he allegedly assaulted an elderly couple inside a crowded bus in Behala following a dispute over a seat reserved for senior citizens.The incident occurred around 9.30 pm on Monday on a bus plying on route 18A (Howrah-Greenfield City) along Diamond Harbour Road. The bus was heading towards Greenfield City and was less than a kilometre from Taratala when the altercation broke out.Chittaranjan Naskar (64), a resident of Gangadharpur in Maheshtala, alleged that copassenger Dhurba Sinha of Shyam Sundar Pally in Parnashree started the argument over the senior citizen seat.The dispute intensified near Pathakpara, close to Behala PS, and soon turned violent.Cops said Sinha allegedly punched Naskar’s wife, Durga Mondal, on the forehead, causing her to lose consciousness briefly. When Naskar protested, he too was assaulted. Both suffered bleeding injuries, allegedly caused by rings worn by the accused.Passengers raised an alarm and the bus was stopped. The injured couple were taken to Vidyasagar State General Hospital and later discharged, said DC (Behala-SW) Rahul De.Following Naskar’s complaint, Behala police arrested Sinha and registered a case of assault. He was granted conditional bail by a local court on Tuesday.
Article Summary: A 25-year-old man was arrested after he allegedly assaulted an elderly couple inside a crowded bus in Behala following a dispute over a seat reserved for senior citizens. The incident occurred around 9.30 pm on Monday on a bus plying on route 18A (Howrah-Greenfield City) along Diamond Harbour Road.
